name: Angel Food Pudding Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 325°F (165°C). Grease a 9-inch angel food cake pan.
2. In a medium bowl, sift together the flour, cream of tartar, and salt.
3. In another large bowl, whip the egg whites with an electric mixer on high speed until they become foamy. Gradually add the sugar while continuing to whip at high speed until stiff peaks form.
4. Gently fold the dry ingredients into the whipped egg whites using a rubber spatula until combined.
5. Pour the batter into the prepared pan and bake for 20-25 minutes, or until the top is firm and crisp and a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean.
6. Allow the cake to cool completely in the pan on a wire rack.
7. Once cooled, carefully cut off about 1 inch from the top of the cake using a sharp knife, being careful not to damage the sides.
8. Using a serrated knife, cut approximately 1 inch deep into the side of the cake, being careful not to cut through the bottom layer.
9. Scoop out the center portion of the cake and discard it in the trash.
10. In a separate bowl, mix together 1 package (15 ounces) of chocolate pudding with 1 cup of whipped cream according to package directions.
11. Gently fold the pudding mixture into the remaining angel food cake until well combined.
12. Transfer the filling into the prepared shell and spread evenly.
13. Replace the top layer of the cake and gently smooth the surface with a spatula, spreading additional whipped cream on top if desired.
14. Chill the cake in the refrigerator for at least 2 hours before serving to allow it to set.
Note: This recipe is designed to be low-calorie, using sugar-free pudding and minimal added ingredients. The exact nutritional content will depend on the specific brands of products used.
